_comment: Editable source for the scholarly-search demo. Run gen_embeddings.py to (re)generate ../data.cql and ../queries.cql with real nomic-embed-text-v2-moe embeddings. Three topic clusters (consensus, vector-search, storage) make ANN meaningful; author names have phonetic variants; citations form a graph; daily_counts make papers 3 and 8 'trend'.

papers.title: Paxos Made Practical
papers.abstract: We present a practical treatment of the Paxos consensus protocol for building fault-tolerant replicated state machines. Replicas reach agreement on a single value despite crashes and message loss using a majority quorum.

papers.title: Raft: Understandable Distributed Consensus
papers.abstract: Raft is a consensus algorithm for managing a replicated log. It is designed to be more understandable than Paxos through leader election, log replication, and strong safety, while providing the same fault tolerance and performance.

papers.title: Accord: Strict-Serializable Distributed Transactions
papers.abstract: Accord is a leaderless protocol for multi-partition transactions that guarantees strict serializability. It orders conflicting transactions with hybrid logical clocks and a fast-path quorum, avoiding dedicated coordinators.

papers.title: Quorum Replication Under Network Partitions
papers.abstract: We analyze how quorum-based replication trades availability for consistency when the network partitions. Tunable read and write quorums let operators choose where to sit on the CAP spectrum.

papers.title: HNSW: Hierarchical Navigable Small World Graphs
papers.abstract: Hierarchical Navigable Small World graphs provide efficient and robust approximate nearest neighbor search in high-dimensional vector spaces. A layered proximity graph yields logarithmic search complexity with strong recall.

papers.title: Product Quantization for Billion-Scale Similarity Search
papers.abstract: Product quantization compresses high-dimensional vectors into compact codes, enabling billion-scale approximate similarity search in memory. We decompose the space into subspaces and quantize each independently.

papers.title: Semantic Retrieval with Dense Text Embeddings
papers.abstract: Dense embeddings from transformer encoders map queries and documents into a shared vector space so that semantic similarity becomes nearest-neighbor search. This outperforms lexical retrieval on paraphrased queries.

papers.title: Scalar Quantization for Memory-Efficient Vector Search
papers.abstract: We quantize embedding vectors to 8-bit and 4-bit scalars to shrink the index, then rerank a small candidate set with full-precision distances. This recovers most of the recall at a fraction of the memory and latency.

papers.title: The Log-Structured Merge Tree
papers.abstract: The LSM tree buffers writes in memory and flushes sorted runs to disk, merging them in the background. It trades read amplification for high write throughput, the basis of modern key-value storage engines.

papers.title: Compaction Strategies for Write-Heavy Workloads
papers.abstract: We compare size-tiered, leveled, and time-window compaction for LSM storage engines under write-heavy workloads, quantifying write amplification, space amplification, and read latency tradeoffs.

papers.title: Disaggregated Storage on Cloud Object Stores
papers.abstract: We move durable storage to a cloud object store and keep local disk as an ephemeral cache, writing behind asynchronously. Compute scales independently of data, eliminating rebalancing on cluster growth.

papers.title: Caching and Tiering for Cloud-Native Databases
papers.abstract: A cloud-native database caches hot pages on local NVMe and serves cold data from object storage. Pinning policies and tiered eviction keep tail latency low while minimizing storage cost.

queries.name: ann_efficient_nn
queries.text: memory-efficient approximate nearest neighbor search over embeddings
